author: Mohamed Diomande
name: Anticipatory Transformer V2: MoE Trajectory Routing + Entropy-Weighted Loss
blurb: Builds on SOTA stack (10L, Int5-MLP/Int6-Attn, BigramHash 10240, SmearGate, OrthoInit, MuonWD, SWA, sliding window eval, zstd-22) with three innovations from anticipation geometry: (1) MoE trajectory routing with 10 specialist experts, each mapping 7 hidden-state-derived scalars to per-head attention modulation. Routing via learned centroids, zero-parameter at routing time. (2) Entropy-weighted training loss (alpha=0.15) focusing gradient on high-uncertainty tokens. (3) 7-scalar trajectory extraction (commitment, uncertainty, transition_pressure, recovery_margin, phase_stiffness, novelty, stability) from hidden state dynamics. Total MoE overhead: ~6.9K params, ~5% compute. torch.compile(fullgraph=True) verified stable.

smoke_test.note: 2000-step smoke test — validates training stability only, not competition score

notes: Validated on 8x H100 80GB HBM3 (Nebraska, Vast.ai instance 34969344). Seed 1337 complete: val_bpb=1.14718941 (final_int8_zlib_roundtrip_exact). Seeds 42 and 7 running. Step count: 6338 steps in 600s (94.67ms/step, MoE overhead). SWA: 24 checkpoints averaged. Submission size: 16,558,338 bytes (model 16,537,330 + code 21,008). torch.compile(fullgraph=True) stable — no crashes, no NaN across 6338 steps. All innovations toggleable: MOE_TRAJECTORY_ENABLED, ENTROPY_LOSS_ENABLED, TRAJECTORY_ATTN_ENABLED. Code compressed LZMA-9+base64: 63.7KB -> 20.5KB (67.8% reduction).
